workAreas.title: Frontend
workAreas.description: Interfaces, design systems, browser work, and product polish.

workAreas.title: Backend
workAreas.description: Systems, data, security, and runtime operations.

workAreas.title: Mobile
workAreas.description: Native apps, React Native, device testing, and mobile delivery.

workAreas.title: Workflow
workAreas.description: Files, docs, planning, and release work.

workAreas.title: Agent Engineering
workAreas.description: Prompts, tools, evaluation, orchestration, and agent runtime design.

collections.title: Starter Pack
collections.description: First-install skills for Laravel, PHP, React, TypeScript, and CSS work on this project.

skills.name: webapp-testing
skills.description: Toolkit for interacting with and testing local web applications using Playwright. Supports verifying frontend functionality, debugging UI behavior, capturing browser screenshots, and viewing browser logs.
skills.author: anthropics
skills.whyHere: Complements Playwright with a practical local web app testing workflow for UI regressions.

skills.name: frontend-design
skills.description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications (examples include websites, landing pages, dashboards, React components, HTML/CSS layouts, or when styling/beautifying any web UI). Generates creative, polished code and UI design that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
skills.author: anthropics
skills.whyHere: Anchors React, TypeScript, and CSS interface work with production-grade UI craft and visual polish.

skills.name: playwright
skills.description: Use when the task requires automating a real browser from the terminal (navigation, form filling, snapshots, screenshots, data extraction, UI-flow debugging) via `playwright-cli` or the bundled wrapper script.
skills.author: openai
skills.whyHere: Adds browser automation for validating React and TypeScript UI behavior against real pages.

skills.name: shadcn
skills.description: Manages shadcn components and projects — adding, searching, fixing, debugging, styling, and composing UI. Provides project context, component docs, and usage examples. Applies when working with shadcn/ui, component registries, presets, --preset codes, or any project with a components.json file. Also triggers for "shadcn init", "create an app with --preset", or "switch to --preset".
skills.author: shadcn-ui
skills.whyHere: Gives the frontend shelf a concrete component workflow for React UI composition and styling decisions.

skills.name: security-best-practices
skills.description: Perform language and framework specific security best-practice reviews and suggest improvements. Trigger only when the user explicitly requests security best practices guidance, a security review/report, or secure-by-default coding help. Trigger only for supported languages (python, javascript/typescript, go). Do not trigger for general code review, debugging, or non-security tasks.
skills.author: openai
skills.whyHere: Anchors the backend shelf with framework-aware security review habits for PHP/Laravel auth, validation, and data handling.

skills.name: cloudflare-deploy
skills.description: Deploy applications and infrastructure to Cloudflare using Workers, Pages, and related platform services. Use when the user asks to deploy, host, publish, or set up a project on Cloudflare.
skills.author: openai
skills.whyHere: Covers deployment and runtime checks useful for hosting-oriented PHP/React projects that may sit behind Cloudflare.

skills.name: security-threat-model
skills.description: Repository-grounded threat modeling that enumerates trust boundaries, assets, attacker capabilities, abuse paths, and mitigations, and writes a concise Markdown threat model. Trigger only when the user explicitly asks to threat model a codebase or path, enumerate threats/abuse paths, or perform AppSec threat modeling. Do not trigger for general architecture summaries, code review, or non-security design work.
skills.author: openai
skills.whyHere: Adds repo-grounded threat modeling for Laravel APIs, auth boundaries, database access, and hosting control-plane risk.
